inline_comments.message: Do we need to logs here?
I think good if its debug level
Error retrieving resource data for cm handle:

The heading of this test says that it is UNSYNCHRONISED and READY. 
Either change the heading or add a variable and edit the heading accordingly
The haselcast starts slowly, os we need extra 100 miliseconds before the first request

Is it possible to make any updates to the Map here ? if this method only sets the boolean to true, it would be better to rename the method to something that depicts the expected change.
Also I was wondering why do we want to add it with a false flag first(line 92) and then come and update here. Why not add it here with a true flag?

Do we need the returned list to be immutable?
If not then we could just check for non emptiness of te list and then shuffle the collection.
return yangModelCmHandles at the end as done in LNo 112.
Query : why do we need to shuffle?
the getCmHandleDataNodesByCpsPath has been updated to include /ancestor::cm-handles
Same here, needs to be removed, creating false positive
List out some of the changes you made or what you would like to be highlighted from this commit
Is this check necessary? Is the yang models cm handle not defined as empty list by default?
Yes, it is true, it is not necessary any more.
NB , dont need this in groovy

NB: use local constants to convey meaning
e.g.
 final static boolean MODEL_SYNC_IN_PROGRESS = false
 final static boolean MODEL_SYNC_DONE = true
why did you get rid of the private method with a good name that was used twice?
if the query takes in a single cm handle ID i should not/ cannot return a list
I'm confused about this logic, can we have a chat please
not sure for te need of 'map' in teh names (it is against our convention).. Just moduleSyncSemaphores would do I think

I think this,method is only used to check if the given cm-handle(id) is in the given state.
so instead of returning a list the method (in CmHandleQueries) should be renamed and return a boolean


e.g. boolean cmHandleHasState(cmHandleId, cmHandleState)
same conversion as on line #2 so restore using private method
see proposed scenario strings above comment
Can we not leave this as the interface?
not sure about adding map as the underlying implementation could change?
ConcurrentMap is an interface too, but it is necessary to match with the static type in the watchdog class.
This is the correct behavior, we mention this problem during the implementation proposal, and we decided first try all cmHandle once and after it, in the next 30 min cycle try the faileds again.
NB: should be plures i.e. add a 's'
NB: should be plures i.e. add a 's'
so wear are still executing a query?! Do yu hope to improve this in the next iteration?

agreed the next time the watchdog will wake up this entry wil be attempted again.
I think it is a rare edge case and no need for complex code to speed this scenario up
NB: should be plures i.e. add a 's'
NB: should be plures i.e. add a 's'
NB: should be plures i.e. add a 's'
NB: should be plures i.e. add a 's'
Yes, I will make it in another commit.
NB: insert 'a data node representing the'  (like above method)
NB: a data node representing the cm handle
in this case as it is a get it should be xpath instead of cpspath

note from Toine. I decided to rename all get-operations that are using cpsPath QUERIES to be prefixed with 'query' so it is more obvious that this is a more costly query then a simple (index using) xpath get operation in the DB!
NB: Private method should come after all public methods

NB: update the label of test that are now testing 'query'-methods
ie. start label with 'Query' instead 'Get'
(p.s. a nice more generic word is used below on line #124 'Retrieve' :-)
whatever you prefer it is Non-Blocking comment anyway)

Toine, Sourabh : Here we have the fix for the new bug which Toine opened.
Correct?

this should be reverted to convertCmHandlesDataNodesToYangModelCmHandles(advisedCmHandlesAsDataNodeList);
